skill.name: vue
skill.description: Vue 3 guidance for components, reactivity, Composition API, TypeScript, composables, slots, lifecycle hooks, and async patterns.
skill.summary: Build Vue 3 applications with Composition API, TypeScript, and documented component patterns.
skill.author: antfu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. Backticks, dynamic imports, fetch calls, and Vue terminology appear inside Markdown reference material and do not show command execution, exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from community-authored documentation snippets.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick detections point to Markdown inline code and fenced Vue examples. No executable Ruby, shell command, command substitution, or runtime invocation is present at the reviewed locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visibly Markdown syntax and Vue sample code. I found no command execution API or shell invocation in these reviewed locations.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Imports Are Local Vue Async Component Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import detections document Vue async component loading from a local component path. They are not executed by the skill and do not import remote code or user-controlled module names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports use a fixed local path inside a fenced TypeScript example. There is no evidence that the skill runs this code.

low_findings.title: Network Calls Are Example Fetches And Source Links
low_findings.description: The network detections are local API fetch examples and links to official Vue documentation. They do not send secrets, contact suspicious hosts, or run as part of the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed calls use relative URLs or documentation URLs in Markdown examples. I found no credential collection or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: High-Severity Static Blockers Are Vue Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts align with words in Vue documentation, such as ref, Error, Date, and debug hook names. The reviewed lines describe framework concepts rather than cryptographic code or system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is Vue reference prose and type examples, not cryptographic routines or host reconnaissance. No malicious intent is evident.

content.user_title: Build Vue 3 Components with Composition API
content.value_statement: Vue projects need consistent patterns for state, components, and TypeScript. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused Vue 3 references for practical implementation work.
actual_capabilities: Guides Vue 3 component work with script setup and TypeScript patterns.
Explains ref, reactive, computed, watch, and watchEffect usage.
Covers props, emits, slots, v-model, and lifecycle hooks.
Documents composables, custom directives, and template refs.
Provides guidance for provide/inject, async components, and Suspense.
Points to Vue documentation references for each covered topic.
limitations: Does not execute or test Vue application code.
Does not include routing, Pinia, Nuxt, or build-tool setup guidance.
Focuses on Vue 3.5 and may not fit older Vue 2 projects.
Examples are reference snippets and may need adaptation for a real app.

use_cases.title: Create Typed Components
use_cases.description: Build Vue components with typed props, emits, slots, and v-model patterns.

use_cases.title: Refactor Shared Logic
use_cases.description: Move repeated stateful behavior into composables that preserve reactivity and cleanup side effects.

use_cases.title: Review Vue Patterns
use_cases.description: Check whether component code follows Composition API, lifecycle, and async handling guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Vue 3 component using script setup and TypeScript. Include typed props, a simple computed value, and clear template structure.

prompt_templates.title: Add Component Events
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this Vue component to use typed defineEmits and defineModel where appropriate. Explain the parent usage in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Extract a Composable
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this repeated Vue logic into a composable. Preserve reactivity after destructuring and include cleanup for side effects.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Async Vue Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Vue async component flow. Identify Suspense requirements, loading and error states, watcher cleanup, and safer alternatives.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for a typed profile card component.
output_examples.output: The skill helps produce a Vue 3 component plan with typed props, computed display state, emitted actions, and parent integration notes.

output_examples.input: A team has duplicated fetch and loading logic across several components.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests a composable structure with reactive return values, error state, loading state, and cleanup guidance.

output_examples.input: A reviewer is unsure whether top-level await is appropriate.
output_examples.output: The skill highlights the Suspense boundary requirement and offers an onMounted loading pattern when explicit states are better.

best_practices: Use script setup with TypeScript for new Vue 3 components.
Prefer refs for destructurable state and return plain objects from composables.
Clean up watchers, timers, and event listeners created by reusable logic.
anti_patterns: Do not destructure reactive objects when the destructured values must stay reactive.
Do not use top-level await without planning the required Suspense boundary.
Do not mix untyped props and emits when component contracts are important.

faq.question: Which Vue version does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Vue 3.5 and newer patterns, including script setup and reactive props destructure.

faq.question: Does it cover Vue 2?
faq.answer: No. The references focus on Vue 3 and the Composition API.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript components?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes typed props, emits, template refs, component instances, and generic component patterns.

faq.question: Does it include state management libraries?
faq.answer: No. It covers Vue reactivity and composables, not Pinia or Vuex.

faq.question: Can it review existing Vue code?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare code against the included component, reactivity, lifecycle, and async guidance.

faq.question: Does the skill run build commands?
faq.answer: No. It is documentation-based and does not execute project commands by itself.
